name: Response quality check
description: Uses the LLM to verify the response is helpful and on-topic rather than evasive or harmful.

versions.content: Require citations for material factual claims when a retrieval source is available.
Example validation: reject answers that mention sources but do not include a citation marker.
LLM rubric: verify the answer makes supported factual claims only and flags unsupported statements.

You are a quality reviewer. Evaluate whether the following response adequately and helpfully answers a user's request.

Response: "{{text}}"

Reply with only "true" if the response is helpful and on-topic, or "false" if it is unhelpful, evasive, harmful, or refuses to engage with a reasonable request.

name: Secrets and length check
description: Fails if the output contains an API key pattern or exceeds 1500 words.

versions.content: Example validation: reject outputs that contain secrets, API keys, or access tokens.

name: Refusal detection
description: Fails if the LLM response begins with a refusal phrase, indicating it did not attempt to answer.

versions.content: (?i)^(I'm sorry[,.]|I cannot |I can't |I am unable to |Unfortunately[,.]? I (cannot|can't|am unable))

name: Minimum response content
description: Passes when the output contains at least 5 words, ensuring the model produced a real answer.

versions.content:
text = output.strip()
return len(text) >= 20 and len(text.split()) >= 5
